about.title: A premium mentor’s approach to English: precise, practical, and personally accountable.
about.description: Faris combines formal training, real teaching repetition, and clear performance feedback to create an experience that feels calm, intelligent, and genuinely developmental.

pillars.title: Structured guidance
pillars.description: Every learner needs a roadmap. Sessions are designed around clear goals, visible correction, and a better sense of what to improve next.

pillars.title: Human confidence-building
pillars.description: Progress in English is not only technical. Learners also need a trainer who helps them feel steady, prepared, and capable when it matters.

pillars.title: Practical refinement
pillars.description: From pronunciation and grammar to speaking rhythm and presentation delivery, the work stays rooted in real use rather than abstract theory.

services.title: Premium training paths for real-world communication goals.
services.description: Each service is built to move learners from hesitation to composed performance, whether the goal is IELTS, business communication, interview success, or stronger spoken fluency.

items.title: Spoken English Training
items.description: A speaking-centered path for learners who want smoother daily conversation, better sentence control, and stronger listening response.

items.title: Business English
items.description: Professional communication support for meetings, presentations, workplace conversations, and high-stakes interactions.

items.title: IELTS Preparation
items.description: Targeted preparation for learners who want a structured route to stronger speaking, writing, reading, and listening performance.

items.title: Interview Preparation
items.description: Practice and refinement for learners preparing for English-based interviews, admissions, and competitive opportunities.

items.title: Communication Skills
items.description: A broader communication offering for learners who need stronger articulation, explanation, and professional expression.

items.title: Pronunciation Coaching
items.description: Focused work on clarity, mouth placement, connected speech, and speaking habits that affect credibility and ease.

items.title: English for Professionals
items.description: A tailored route for people whose careers require consistent, credible English across meetings, interviews, and written communication.

items.title: Confidence Coaching
items.description: A supportive but demanding track for learners who know the issue is not only knowledge, but hesitation, self-doubt, and fear of mistakes.

why.title: A learning relationship built on standards, refinement, and calm pressure.
why.description: This is not mass-market tutoring. The experience is designed to feel guided, personalized, and measurably useful from the first session onward.

items.title: Feedback that actually changes performance
items.description: Corrections are precise, actionable, and connected to what the learner needs next rather than vague encouragement alone.

items.title: A plan that respects your goal
items.description: Whether the target is IELTS, business communication, or stronger spoken fluency, the training path stays aligned with that objective.

items.title: Serious support without coldness
items.description: The tone is warm, but the standard remains high. Learners feel supported without being under-challenged.

items.title: Practical outcomes over decorative theory
items.description: Every session aims to improve what the learner can actually say, understand, present, or express in the real world.

items.title: Confidence built through repetition and clarity
items.description: Improvement is not left to inspiration. It is developed through deliberate, well-explained practice.

credentials.title: Training credibility supported by study, repetition, and professional discipline.
credentials.description: Public profile details point to a coach who has invested in formal development as well as real classroom repetition.

items.title: Cambridge and TESOL-backed foundation
items.description: The training background reflects both Cambridge teacher development and TESOL study, giving the methodology a solid professional base.

items.title: Years of active teaching across levels
items.description: Experience spans beginners, intermediate learners, advanced learners, adults, teens, and practical communication goals.

items.title: A learner’s perspective on growth
items.description: Because Faris built his own English to a high level, the coaching style stays empathetic, realistic, and tuned to learner obstacles.

testimonials.title: Public reviews that point to calm structure, real support, and visible progress.
testimonials.description: These review themes are adapted from public feedback on Preply and presented here in a concise editorial format.

items.quote: I reached an overall IELTS band 7 and felt supported at every stage of the process.
items.name: Alia

items.quote: Lessons felt structured and professional, with detailed corrections that made weekly improvement easy to notice.
items.name: Tamer

items.quote: He understands the struggle of learning English as a non-native speaker and gives practical feedback that really helps.

philosophy.quote: Fluent English is not only about knowing more words. It is about becoming clearer, calmer, and more persuasive when your voice matters.
philosophy.supporting: The training philosophy is simple: build command through disciplined practice, honest correction, and the kind of mentorship that turns effort into visible communication maturity.

audiences.title: Built for learners with serious goals and high standards.
audiences.description: The site speaks to more than one audience, but every path is designed for people who care about meaningful progress rather than casual exposure alone.

items.title: Adults improving spoken English
items.description: For learners who want to sound more composed, capable, and natural in daily and professional conversation.

items.title: Professionals needing business communication
items.description: For ambitious professionals who need English that feels credible in meetings, interviews, and workplace communication.

items.title: IELTS learners
items.description: For test-takers who want structure, clarity, and strong feedback across all four IELTS skills.

items.title: University students
items.description: For students who need stronger academic communication, clearer speaking, and better performance in English-medium environments.

items.title: Teens with high potential
items.description: For younger learners who benefit from premium support, accountability, and confidence-building speaking work.

items.title: Parents seeking serious support
items.description: For families who want disciplined, high-quality English guidance for a child or teenager with real potential.

faq.title: Common questions from serious learners and families.
faq.description: A concise guide to how the training relationship usually works.

items.question: Who are these sessions best suited for?
items.answer: They are ideal for adults, professionals, IELTS learners, university students, teens, and families looking for a premium standard of English support.

items.question: Can the sessions focus mainly on speaking confidence?
items.answer: Yes. Speaking confidence, pronunciation, fluency, and sentence control are core strengths of the training approach.

items.question: Is the approach suitable for professionals?
items.answer: Yes. Business communication, interviews, presentation delivery, and English for workplace credibility are central service areas.

items.question: Will I receive detailed feedback?
items.answer: That is one of the defining strengths of the experience. Learners can expect direct feedback on structure, pronunciation, grammar, and practical speaking performance.

items.question: What if I am motivated but still hesitant to speak?
items.answer: That is a common starting point. The process is designed to convert hesitation into clearer speaking habits through guided repetition and calm correction.

finalCta.title: Choose a training relationship that treats your English with seriousness and care.
finalCta.description: If you want articulate spoken English, stronger professional communication, or a more confident voice in important situations, begin with a focused inquiry.

hero.title: A trainer’s story shaped by study, discipline, and the belief that practice builds real language power.
hero.description: Faris Rashad’s work sits at the intersection of language teaching, communication refinement, and personal confidence-building. The result is an experience that feels both intellectually serious and deeply human.
asideItems: Practical communication gains over generic lesson flow

story.title: From language learner to communication mentor.
paragraphs: Faris openly speaks about building his own English from zero to a high level. That personal journey informs a teaching style that understands hesitation, inconsistency, and the emotional side of language learning as well as the technical one.

His academic and professional development point to a trainer who values method. Study in English education, TESOL exposure, and Cambridge teacher training combine with years of active teaching across multiple learner profiles.
This gives the brand a distinctive tone: scholarly without being distant, practical without becoming mechanical, and premium without ever feeling performative.

philosophy.title: Three principles behind the work.
philosophy.description: Every serious mentor needs a philosophy. These principles shape the learning environment and the way each session is delivered.

items.title: Practice before performance
items.description: Confidence comes from repeated, well-structured use of language, not from passive exposure alone.

items.title: Correction with clarity
items.description: Feedback should reduce confusion, not create it. Learners need to understand what changed and why it matters.

items.title: Growth that respects the person
items.description: A premium learning experience takes the learner’s psychology seriously. Standards remain high, but the relationship never becomes punitive.

standards.title: The experience is designed to feel prepared, intentional, and calm.
standards.description: Professional standards are not an accessory. They shape the learner’s trust in the process.

items.title: Session preparation
items.description: Each lesson is expected to carry a purpose, not simply consume time.

items.title: Visible learner progress
items.description: Progress should be felt in clarity, confidence, and communication outcomes, not only in textbook completion.

items.title: High-quality correction
items.description: Mistakes become useful when they are explained well and linked to future performance.

items.title: Respect for learner ambition
items.description: Serious learners deserve a trainer who treats their goals with focus and rigor.

whyItWorks.title: A premium methodology depends on both structure and tone.
whyItWorks.description: These factors help learners stay engaged long enough to produce real transformation.
points: Learners know what they are working on and why it matters.
Feedback is specific enough to guide the next improvement step.
Speaking work is treated as a trainable skill, not a mystery.
Confidence is built through rehearsal, not empty reassurance.
The experience feels disciplined without becoming cold or generic.

cta.title: If you want a trainer who balances refinement with real support, start the conversation here.
cta.description: The next step can be a booking inquiry, a service review, or a direct contact message depending on what fits your goal best.

hero.title: A premium suite of English and communication services built around real performance.
hero.description: These services are designed for learners who want stronger speech, cleaner communication, and a trainer who can translate ambition into steady progress.
asideItems: More composed speaking in professional settings
Smarter preparation for tests, interviews, and presentations

catalog.title: Eight focused tracks, each built for a distinct learner need.
catalog.description: All services share the same premium teaching posture, but each one targets a different communication challenge.

items.title: Spoken English Training
items.description: A speaking-heavy route that improves fluency, listening response, everyday expression, and verbal confidence.

items.title: Business English
items.description: Workplace-focused coaching for meetings, presentations, interviews, and English that needs to sound credible in professional environments.

items.title: IELTS Preparation
items.description: A targeted preparation path covering speaking, writing, reading, and listening with organized feedback and score-aware strategy.

items.title: Interview Preparation
items.description: Support for job interviews, scholarship interviews, and opportunities where English answers need to feel thoughtful and confident.

items.title: Communication Skills
items.description: Training that sharpens explanation, persuasion, and how ideas are delivered when learners need to sound more organized and articulate.

items.title: Pronunciation Coaching
items.description: Precision work on sound clarity, connected speech, rhythm, and speaking habits that influence intelligibility and confidence.

items.title: English for Professionals
items.description: A practical track for learners whose growth depends on English performance at work, in applications, and in visible professional moments.

items.title: Confidence Coaching
items.description: Guided work for learners who have knowledge but still hesitate, freeze, or lose control when they need English most.

process.title: What the training experience usually looks like.
process.description: The process stays simple, premium, and purpose-driven.

steps.title: Assess the current level and goal
steps.description: The work begins with clarity: what the learner needs, what currently blocks progress, and what success should look like.

steps.title: Build the training structure
steps.description: Sessions are organized around the right balance of speaking, correction, skill-building, and contextual practice.

steps.title: Refine through feedback and repetition
steps.description: Progress is reinforced through targeted feedback, repeated use, and adjustments based on real performance.

fit.title: Different goals require different language pressure.
fit.description: The service selection should match the learner’s most important communication environment.

groups.title: Professionals and corporate learners
groups.description: Best served by Business English, English for Professionals, communication skills, and interview preparation.

groups.title: Exam-focused learners
groups.description: Best served by IELTS preparation with structured strategy and feedback across the tested skills.

groups.title: Fluency and confidence learners
groups.description: Best served by spoken English training, pronunciation coaching, and confidence-focused support.

groups.title: Parents and ambitious students
groups.description: Best served by a tailored mix that balances accountability, encouragement, and steady speaking development.

cta.title: Not sure which path fits best? Start with a focused inquiry and match the service to the goal.
cta.description: That first conversation can clarify whether the need is fluency, business communication, IELTS, pronunciation, interviews, or a broader confidence problem.

themes.title: What strong learner feedback tends to have in common.
themes.description: Across different audiences and goals, the same transformation themes appear again and again.

items.title: Structure reduces confusion
items.description: Learners feel calmer when the work is organized and the next improvement step is clear.

items.title: Feedback makes progress visible
items.description: Corrections on pronunciation, grammar, and vocabulary become a map for real weekly growth.

items.title: Encouragement supports performance
items.description: Warmth matters when learners are trying to speak more bravely and recover from mistakes faster.

philosophy.quote: A strong review is not the end goal. The real goal is a learner who sounds more capable in the moments that shape their future.
philosophy.supporting: That is why the work prioritizes durable communication gains instead of shallow lesson completion.
